intro.text: HicareNet, Inc. ("HicareNet," "we," "us," or "our"), a California-based subsidiary of Hicare Co., Ltd., is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal information. This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use our Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM), Chronic Care Management (CCM), Annual Wellness Visit (AWV), and Value-Based Care (VBC) services, or visit our website.

personal.title: Personal Information
personal.text: We may collect the following personal information when you interact with our services:
personal.item1: Name, address, email address, and phone number
personal.item2: Date of birth, gender, and other demographic information
personal.item4: Professional credentials and organizational affiliations (for healthcare providers)

health.title: Protected Health Information (PHI)
health.text: In the course of providing our healthcare IT services, we may process:
health.item1: Vital signs and biometric data collected through RPM devices (blood pressure, glucose levels, weight, pulse oximetry, temperature)
health.item2: Chronic condition management records and care plans
health.item3: Clinical notes, diagnoses, and treatment information

usage.title: Usage and Technical Data
usage.text: We automatically collect certain information when you visit our website or use our platform:
usage.item1: IP address, browser type, operating system, and device identifiers
usage.item2: Pages visited, time spent, and navigation patterns
usage.item4: Log data and error reports for service improvement

use.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
use.intro: We use the information we collect for the following purposes:
use.item1: Providing and managing Remote Patient Monitoring and Chronic Care Management services
use.item2: Facilitating communication between patients and healthcare providers
use.item3: Processing billing, insurance claims, and payment transactions
use.item4: Improving our platform, services, and user experience through analytics
use.item5: Complying with legal obligations, including HIPAA and state healthcare regulations
use.item7: Conducting quality assurance and clinical outcome analysis
use.item8: Responding to inquiries, support requests, and feedback

hipaa.title: 3. HIPAA Compliance
hipaa.intro: As a healthcare technology provider, HicareNet is committed to full compliance with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) and the HITECH Act.

phi.title: PHI Handling
phi.text: All Protected Health Information processed through our platform is handled in strict accordance with HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules. We implement administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of PHI.

baa.title: Business Associate Agreements
baa.text: We enter into Business Associate Agreements (BAAs) with all covered entities and subcontractors who handle PHI through our platform. These agreements define the permitted uses and disclosures of PHI and establish obligations for safeguarding health information.

minimum.title: Minimum Necessary Standard
minimum.text: We apply the minimum necessary standard to all uses, disclosures, and requests for PHI. Access to PHI is limited to authorized personnel who require it to perform their designated functions, and is controlled through role-based access controls.

sharing.title: 4. Data Sharing and Disclosure
sharing.intro: We do not sell your personal information. We may share your information only in the following circumstances:
sharing.item1: Healthcare Providers: To facilitate treatment, care coordination, and clinical operations as authorized
sharing.item2: Service Providers: With trusted third-party vendors who assist in operating our platform, subject to confidentiality obligations and BAAs where applicable
sharing.item3: Legal Requirements: When required by law, regulation, legal process, or governmental request
sharing.item4: Business Transfers: In connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, with appropriate safeguards
sharing.item5: With Consent: When you have provided explicit authorization for a specific disclosure

security.title: 5. Data Security
security.intro: We employ comprehensive security measures to protect your information:
security.item1: Encryption: All data in transit is protected with TLS 1.2 or higher. Data at rest is encrypted using AES-256 encryption
security.item2: ISO 27001 Certification: Our information security management system is certified to the ISO 27001 international standard
security.item3: ISO 27701 Certification: Our privacy information management system meets the ISO 27701 standard for data privacy
security.item4: Access Controls: Multi-factor authentication, role-based access, and regular access reviews
security.item5: Monitoring: Continuous security monitoring, vulnerability assessments, and regular penetration testing
security.item6: Incident Response: Documented breach notification procedures in compliance with HIPAA and applicable state laws

rights.title: 6. Your Rights
rights.intro: You have the following rights regarding your personal information and PHI:
rights.item1: Right to Access: You may request a copy of the personal information we hold about you
rights.item2: Right to Correction: You may request correction of inaccurate or incomplete information
rights.item3: Right to Deletion: You may request deletion of your personal information, subject to legal retention requirements
rights.item4: Right to Restriction: You may request that we restrict certain processing of your information
rights.item5: Right to an Accounting of Disclosures: You may request a record of certain disclosures of your PHI
rights.item6: Right to Opt-Out: You may opt out of non-essential communications at any time
rights.exercise: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at info@hicare.net. We will respond to your request within 30 days as required by applicable law.

cookies.title: 7. Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: Our website uses cookies and similar tracking technologies to enhance your experience:
cookies.item1: Essential Cookies: Required for basic website functionality, including authentication and security
cookies.item2: Analytics Cookies: Help us understand how visitors interact with our website to improve performance
cookies.item3: Preference Cookies: Remember your language selection and other display preferences
cookies.manage: You can manage cookie preferences through your browser settings. Disabling certain cookies may limit your ability to use some features of our website.

retention.title: 8. Data Retention
retention.text: We retain personal information and PHI for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this Privacy Policy, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements. Healthcare records are retained in accordance with HIPAA requirements and applicable state medical record retention laws. When data is no longer needed, it is securely deleted or de-identified.

changes.title: 9. Changes to This Policy
changes.text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technology, legal requirements, or other factors. When we make material changes, we will update the "Last Updated" date at the top of this page and, where required, notify you by email or through a prominent notice on our website. We encourage you to review this Privacy Policy periodically.

contact.title: 10. Contact Information
contact.intro: If you have questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us:
contact.subsidiary: US Subsidiary of Hicare Co., Ltd.
contact.hipaa: For HIPAA-related inquiries or to file a privacy complaint, you may also contact the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services Office for Civil Rights.

intro.text: Welcome to HicareNet. These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the services, website, and platform provided by HicareNet, Inc. ("HicareNet," "we," "us," or "our"), a California-based subsidiary of Hicare Co., Ltd. By accessing or using our services, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, you may not use our services.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.text1: By creating an account, accessing our platform, or using any of our services, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms, as well as our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated herein by reference.
acceptance.text2: If you are using our services on behalf of a healthcare organization, clinic, or other entity, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that entity to these Terms. In such cases, "you" and "your" refer to that entity.

services.title: 2. Description of Services
services.intro: HicareNet provides healthcare information technology services, including but not limited to:
services.item1: Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM): Cloud-based platform and FDA-cleared medical devices for continuous patient monitoring, vital sign collection, and clinical alert management
services.item2: Chronic Care Management (CCM): Comprehensive care coordination platform for managing patients with multiple chronic conditions, including care plan development and patient communication tools
services.item3: Annual Wellness Visit (AWV): Digital tools supporting preventive care visits, health risk assessments, and personalized wellness plans
services.item4: Value-Based Care (VBC): Analytics and reporting platform supporting quality measurement, population health management, and value-based reimbursement models
services.note: Our services are designed for use by licensed healthcare providers, clinics, hospitals, Accountable Care Organizations (ACOs), and Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNFs) operating within the United States.

accounts.title: 3. User Accounts and Responsibilities
accounts.intro: When you create an account with HicareNet, you agree to:
accounts.item1: Provide accurate, current, and complete registration information
accounts.item2: Maintain the security and confidentiality of your login credentials
accounts.item3: Promptly notify us of any unauthorized access or security breach
accounts.item4: Accept responsibility for all activities conducted under your account
accounts.item5: Ensure that all users within your organization receive appropriate training on platform usage and data privacy
accounts.liability: You are solely responsible for ensuring that your use of the platform complies with all applicable laws, regulations, and professional standards, including HIPAA and state healthcare regulations.

acceptable.title: 4. Acceptable Use Policy
acceptable.intro: You agree not to use our services to:
acceptable.item1: Violate any applicable federal, state, or local laws or regulations
acceptable.item2: Access, use, or disclose Protected Health Information (PHI) in violation of HIPAA
acceptable.item3: Attempt to gain unauthorized access to our systems, networks, or other users' accounts
acceptable.item4: Introduce malware, viruses, or any other harmful code into the platform
acceptable.item5: Reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble any portion of the platform
acceptable.item6: Use the platform for any purpose other than its intended healthcare-related functions
acceptable.item7: Resell, sublicense, or redistribute access to the platform without written authorization

ip.title: 5. Intellectual Property
ip.text1: All content, software, technology, designs, trademarks, and other intellectual property associated with HicareNet's platform and services are owned by HicareNet, Inc. or its parent company Hicare Co., Ltd. and are protected by applicable intellectual property laws.
ip.text2: Your use of our services does not grant you any ownership rights to our intellectual property. You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the platform solely for its intended purpose and in accordance with these Terms.
ip.text3: You retain ownership of all data you input into the platform. By using our services, you grant HicareNet a limited license to process, store, and transmit your data solely for the purpose of providing and improving our services.

compliance.title: 6. HIPAA and Healthcare Compliance
compliance.text1: HicareNet operates as a Business Associate under HIPAA. We will enter into a Business Associate Agreement (BAA) with each covered entity that uses our services involving PHI. The BAA will govern our obligations regarding the use, disclosure, and safeguarding of PHI.
compliance.text2: Our platform is designed and maintained to comply with:
compliance.item1: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Security Rule requirements
compliance.item5: Applicable state healthcare privacy and data protection laws
compliance.text3: You are responsible for ensuring that your use of our platform complies with your own HIPAA obligations and that you have obtained all necessary patient consents and authorizations.

liability.title: 7. Limitation of Liability
liability.text1: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, HicareNet shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, data, business opportunities, or goodwill, arising out of or in connection with your use of our services.
liability.text2: Our total aggregate liability for any claims arising from or related to these Terms or your use of our services shall not exceed the total fees paid by you to HicareNet during the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.
liability.text3: Nothing in these Terms shall limit our liability for gross negligence, willful misconduct, or any liability that cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law.

disclaimers.title: 8. Disclaimers
disclaimers.text1: Our services are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis. HicareNet makes no warranties, express or implied, regarding the platform's reliability, accuracy, completeness, or fitness for a particular purpose.
disclaimers.text2: HicareNet is a healthcare technology provider, not a healthcare provider. Our platform and devices are tools designed to assist licensed healthcare professionals in patient care. We do not provide medical advice, diagnoses, or treatment recommendations. All clinical decisions remain the sole responsibility of the licensed healthcare provider.
disclaimers.text3: While we strive for uninterrupted service, we do not guarantee that the platform will be available at all times. Scheduled maintenance, updates, or unforeseen technical issues may temporarily affect availability.

termination.title: 9. Termination
termination.text1: Either party may terminate the service relationship by providing written notice in accordance with the applicable service agreement. We may suspend or terminate your access to the platform immediately if:
termination.item1: You breach any provision of these Terms
termination.item2: Your use poses a security risk to the platform or other users
termination.item3: Required by law or regulatory action
termination.item4: Your account has been inactive for an extended period
termination.text2: Upon termination, we will provide reasonable assistance in exporting your data in accordance with applicable regulations. Our obligation to safeguard PHI will survive termination as required by HIPAA and the applicable BAA.

indemnification.title: 10. Indemnification
indemnification.text: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless HicareNet, its parent company Hicare Co., Ltd., and their respective officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or in connection with your use of the services, your violation of these Terms, or your violation of any applicable law or third-party rights.

governing.title: 11. Governing Law
governing.text1: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, United States, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.
governing.text2: Any disputes arising out of or relating to these Terms or your use of our services shall be resolved exclusively in the state or federal courts located in the State of California. You consent to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts.

changes.title: 12. Changes to Terms
changes.text: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. When we make material changes, we will update the "Last Updated" date and notify you through the platform or by email. Your continued use of the services after the effective date of any changes constitutes your acceptance of the updated Terms. If you do not agree with the revised Terms, you must discontinue using our services.

contact.title: 13. Contact Information
contact.intro: If you have questions or concerns about these Terms of Service, please contact us:
contact.subsidiary: US Subsidiary of Hicare Co., Ltd.

hero.title: Your Data. Our Highest Priority.
hero.desc: HicareNet maintains the most rigorous security standards in healthcare IT — HIPAA compliant, ISO 27001 and ISO 27701 certified — ensuring your patient data is protected at every layer.

certs.label: CERTIFIED STANDARDS
certs.title: Industry-Leading Certifications
certs.desc: Multiple independent certifications validate our commitment to security and privacy across all operations.

cert1.title: HIPAA Compliant
cert1.desc: Full compliance with HIPAA Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules. BAA available for all covered entities.

cert2.title: ISO 27001
cert2.desc: Information Security Management System certified to international ISO 27001 standards. Last audited December 2025.

cert3.title: ISO 27701
cert3.desc: Privacy Information Management System certified, extending ISO 27001 with GDPR and CCPA-aligned privacy controls.

cert4.title: 120+ Devices
cert4.desc: Compatible with over 120 medical peripheral devices, powering comprehensive remote patient monitoring across clinical environments.

hipaa.title: HIPAA Compliance
hipaa.desc: HicareNet maintains full compliance with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, ensuring the highest standards of protection for protected health information (PHI) across all systems and processes.
hipaa.f1: End-to-end PHI encryption with strict role-based access controls
hipaa.f2: Business Associate Agreements (BAA) available for all covered entities
hipaa.f3: Mandatory staff training and annual HIPAA recertification
hipaa.f4: Documented breach notification procedures and incident response plan

iso27001.title: ISO 27001 Certified
iso27001.desc: Our ISMS is certified to ISO 27001, demonstrating systematic management of sensitive information. Annual independent audit by accredited certification body.
iso27001.audit: Certificate No. C844628 · Issued: January 23, 2026 · Valid through January 2029

iso27701.title: ISO 27701 Certified
iso27701.desc: Our PIMS extends ISO 27001 with specific privacy controls, supporting GDPR, CCPA and global privacy regulations. Data subject rights fully supported.
iso27701.audit: Certificate No. C844629 · Issued: January 23, 2026 · Valid through January 2029

security.label: TECHNICAL SAFEGUARDS
security.title: Data Security Practices
security.desc: Multi-layered security controls protect your data at every level of our infrastructure.

enc.title: Encryption
enc.desc: AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.3 for all data in transit. All PHI is encrypted end-to-end with regularly rotated keys.

access.title: Access Control
access.desc: Role-based access control (RBAC) with mandatory multi-factor authentication. Comprehensive audit logs track all data access events.

infra.title: Infrastructure
infra.desc: HIPAA-eligible cloud infrastructure with SOC 2 Type II compliance. Physically secure data centers with 99.99% uptime SLA.

monitor.title: 24/7 Monitoring
monitor.desc: SIEM integration with automated intrusion detection and real-time alerting for anomalous activity patterns.

backup.title: Backup & Recovery
backup.desc: Automated daily backups with geo-redundant storage. Point-in-time recovery and tested disaster recovery plans.

incident.title: Incident Response
incident.desc: 24-hour breach notification commitment. Documented escalation paths and regular tabletop exercises.

faq.label: COMMON QUESTIONS
faq.title: Compliance FAQ
faq.q1: Can HicareNet sign a Business Associate Agreement (BAA)?
faq.a1: Yes. We provide a signed BAA to all covered entities based on the HHS model agreement, reviewed annually by our legal and compliance teams.
faq.a2: Upon signing an NDA, we can share our ISO 27001 and ISO 27701 certification reports, SOC 2 Type II report, and the most recent penetration test summary.
faq.a3: All patient data is stored within the United States on HIPAA-eligible cloud infrastructure with geo-redundant data centers. Data never leaves US jurisdiction.
faq.q4: How does HicareNet handle breach notification?
faq.a4: We commit to notifying affected covered entities within 24 hours of a confirmed PHI breach, with a detailed incident report and remediation steps.

cta.title: Need Our Compliance Documentation?
cta.desc: Request certifications, audit reports, or schedule a security review with our compliance team.

hero.title: Let's Transform Patient Care Together.
hero.desc: Whether you're ready to deploy RPM, exploring CCM options, or need compliance documentation — our team is here to help you take the next step.

form.title: Send Us a Message
form.subtitle: We'll respond within one business day.
form.name: Full Name
form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us about your needs and how we can help...

cta.title: Start with a 30-Min Discovery Call
cta.desc: See how HicareNet's RPM and CCM platform fits your clinical workflow — with no commitment required.

hero.title: The Future of Remote Patient Monitoring: 2026 Trends
hero.desc: Explore how AI-driven analytics, wearable devices, and evolving CMS regulations are reshaping the RPM landscape.

c1.title: 5 RPM Devices That Are Changing Patient Outcomes
c1.desc: A comprehensive review of the latest remote monitoring devices and their clinical impact on chronic disease management programs.

c2.title: Multilingual Patient Engagement: Breaking Language Barriers in CCM
c2.desc: How supporting 7+ languages in chronic care management improves patient adherence and health outcomes.

c3.title: Navigating CMS Value-Based Care Models in 2026

c4.title: Maximizing Annual Wellness Visit Completion Rates

c5.title: FHIR R4 Interoperability: What Providers Need to Know

c6.title: RPM Billing Guide: CPT Codes and Reimbursement Best Practices
c6.desc: A detailed walkthrough of RPM-related CPT codes, documentation requirements, and strategies to maximize reimbursement.

faq.label: COMMON QUESTIONS
faq.title: FAQ
faq.desc: Quick answers to the most common questions about our RPM and CCM platform.
faq.q1: What devices are compatible with the Hicare Hub platform?
faq.a1: Hicare Hub supports 120+ compatible device models including blood pressure monitors, pulse oximeters, weight scales, and glucometers via Bluetooth and cellular connections.
faq.q2: How quickly can we onboard a new practice?
faq.a2: Most practices are fully onboarded within 2–4 weeks, including device provisioning, EHR integration, staff training, and patient enrollment.
faq.q3: Does HicareNet support HL7 FHIR integration?
faq.a3: Yes. Our platform natively supports HL7 FHIR R4 with bi-directional data exchange for Epic, Cerner, and athenahealth. Full FHIR-based EMR integration is planned for H1 2026.
faq.q4: What languages does the patient app support?
faq.a4: The Hicare patient app supports 7 languages: English, Spanish, Korean, Chinese (Simplified), Vietnamese, Tagalog, and Portuguese.
